page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs, which are mapped from 8 physical cores utilizing Intel Hyper-Threading. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 131 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core processing is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. Similarly, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores consistently placing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads and writes also registers in the average tier.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the D16s_v3 is suited for workloads that can effectively parallelize tasks to overcome lower single-core efficiency. Average performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and Passmark database operations indicates viability for web serving and relational database hosting. It is also capable of handling software compilation and light LLM inference for small models, though performance drops to weak levels for medium-sized models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, it offers standard resource density for general-purpose enterprise workloads where multi-threaded capabilities are prioritized over high single-thread clock speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core capabilities, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, rank in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently achieve average tier results. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads, including general-purpose database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, where parallel execution can offset the weaker single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 131 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine balancing sixteen dedicated vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
131 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Weaker single-core and single-threaded processing capabilities
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
